brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +45.1% since Feb 27 — Brent ~$103/bbl (Apr 13 Monday open) — OIL BREAKS $100 AGAIN — Trump Hormuz BLOCKADE effective 14:00 GMT today — Islamabad collapse + IRGC warning = most dangerous 48 hours of ceasefire
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 13 Monday open: ~$102-103/bbl (+7.3% from Apr 10 close of $96.66). WTI ~$97-105 wide intraday range (+5-8%). Oil surged back above $100 as Trump's Hormuz blockade took effect at 14:00 GMT today — 24 hours after Islamabad talks collapsed. IRGC declared any military vessel approaching the Strait a ceasefire violation to be met with 'harsh and decisive' force. Coalition support is thin — UK explicitly declined to participate (Starmer: 'not getting dragged into the war'), Australia says it hasn't been asked. CENTCOM narrowed blockade scope to Iranian ports only (not all Hormuz traffic) — a significant divergence from Trump's Truth Social announcement. Hormuz functionally closed: ~3,200 vessels stranded (800 tankers, 20,000 mariners), war risk insurance ~1% of ship value (8x pre-war). Strait handles ~20% of global oil transit. The prior week's 3.6% S&P rally on ceasefire hopes is unwinding. Source: ICE Futures Europe / Bloomberg / ADNOC / Lloyd's List / Energy Aspects / Trump Truth Social / CENTCOM.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +50.4% since Feb 27 — WTI ~$100/bbl (Apr 13 Monday open) — INTRADAY RANGE $97-105 — Hormuz BLOCKADE effective today — gas at $4.125 — California hits $5.89
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 13 Monday open: ~$97-105/bbl wide intraday range (+5-8% from $95.63 Apr 10 close). Trump's US naval blockade took effect 14:00 GMT today, just 24 hours after Islamabad talks collapsed. CENTCOM narrowed scope to Iranian ports only — a significant divergence from Trump's Truth Social announcement threatening all Hormuz traffic. AAA national average gas $4.125/gal (up 27% since early March — first time above $4 since Aug 2022). California leads at $5.89; Oklahoma lowest at $3.27. March CPI released Apr 10 showed inflation at 3.3% YoY with 0.9% monthly increase — steepest since mid-2022 — driven by energy costs. Markets now price only ~30% probability of a rate cut by December. Ceasefire on Iran front holds ~144 hours but blockade threatens direct IRGC-USN confrontation. Source: NYMEX / AAA / BLS / Bloomberg / Trump Truth Social / CENTCOM.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$1.145 / +38.4% since Feb 27 — $4.125 nationally (AAA Apr 13) — up 27% since early March — Hormuz BLOCKADE effective today will extend upward pressure — California $5.89
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). Current: $4.125/gal nationally (AAA Apr 13) — up 27% since early March and the first time above $4 since August 2022. Most expensive state: California at $5.89; cheapest: Oklahoma at $3.27. Gas crossed $4.00/gallon Apr 2 for first time since Aug 2022. EIA forecasts monthly average peaking ~$4.30 in April. March CPI (released Apr 10) confirmed gasoline prices surged 21.2% in a single month — the largest monthly spike since 1967. Energy overall +10.9% MoM, +12.5% YoY. Trump's Apr 12 Hormuz blockade took effect 14:00 GMT Apr 13 — pump prices expected to continue rising. Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf posted Washington gas station prices on social media, taunting: 'Enjoy the current pump figures... Soon you'll be nostalgic for $4-$5 gas.' Source: AAA / BLS / EIA / AEI.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: -102 pts / -1.5% from pre-war — S&P ~6,775-6,781 (Apr 13 Monday open) — DOWN 0.5-0.6% — VIX SPIKES 11% to 21.30 — Islamabad collapse + Hormuz blockade triggering risk-off reversal
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Apr 13 Monday open: ~6,775-6,781 (-0.5% to -0.6% on day). Markets opened with a clear risk-off tone driven by failed Islamabad talks and blockade announcement. VIX jumped 10.8% to 21.30. Gold ~$4,724/oz (-0.6%); DXY 99.03 (+0.4%); 10Y Treasury 4.317% (+2 bps); Bitcoin ~$70,900 (-2.7% to -3.0%). Energy stocks surged: XOM $157.61 overnight (+3.3%), CVX $192.39 (+2.0%). Defense mixed: LMT ranged $607-$627, RTX ~$201. Airlines diverged: DAL $64.00 (+0.3%), UAL $91.35 (-1.3%). Goldman Sachs missed on FICC Q1, -4% premarket. Prior week's 3.6% S&P rally on ceasefire optimism unwinding as Trump's Hormuz blockade effective 14:00 GMT today and IRGC warns any military vessel approaching Strait will trigger 'harsh and decisive' force. Markets now price only ~30% probability of rate cut by December. Fed meets Apr 28-29. Source: NYSE / Bloomberg / CNBC / BLS / Federal Reserve.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$900M/day — BLOCKADE OPERATIONS BEGIN — effective 14:00 GMT today — mine clearance continues — Bush CSG approaching Gibraltar — Ford breaks post-Vietnam deployment record ~Apr 14
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 45: ~$900M/day — Trump's US naval blockade of Hormuz took effect 14:00 GMT Apr 13, 24 hours after Islamabad talks collapsed. CENTCOM's operational order narrowed scope to Iranian ports only (not all Hormuz traffic) — a significant divergence from Trump's Truth Social announcement. Blockade operations, mine clearance, vessel interdictions raising burn rate. CENTCOM: 'additional U.S. forces, including underwater drones, will join the clearance effort.' Historic Apr 11 transit: USS Frank E. Petersen Jr. (DDG-121) and USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112) initiated mine-clearing. ~30 warships in region; USNI Apr 6: 108 deployed of 291 total. Atlantic Council (Apr 10): 63-71% AWACS fleet deployed, ~33% tanker aircraft, 29-43% THAAD committed. 16 MQ-9 Reapers lost since Epic Fury began. Harvard Prof. Bilmes: $2B/day including all operational costs. ~50,000+ US service members participated. Bush CSG approaching/at Gibraltar. Ford at 293 days, breaks post-Vietnam record (294 days, Lincoln 2019-20) ~Apr 14. Pentagon supplemental now ~$200B requested (71% of Americans oppose). 60-day War Powers deadline: April 29 — 16 days. Congress returned Apr 14. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / AEI / Harvard / CENTCOM / USNI Fleet Tracker / Atlantic Council.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (45 Days)
total_cost.note: ~$51.8B through Day 45 — Hormuz blockade effective today — CSIS: ~$30B — AEI: $22.3-31B — Harvard: $2B/day, $1 trillion long-term — Pentagon requesting ~$200B supplemental
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS: $16.5B through Day 12, ~$30B total. AEI: $22.3-31B for five weeks. Harvard Prof. Bilmes: $2B/day current rate, $1 trillion long-term. WarCosts.org: $35B+. Trump's Hormuz blockade effective Apr 13 14:00 GMT signals escalation rather than drawdown. Pentagon is requesting ~$200 billion in war funding — 71% of Americans oppose the spending (CNN poll). Pew Research (Mar 16-22): 61% disapprove of Trump's handling of Iran conflict; only 37% approve. Six in ten Americans say striking Iran was the 'wrong decision.' Only 29% say the war has been 'worth the toll' (vs 59% for Iraq 2003). YouGov/Economist net approval -19 to -23; Reuters/Ipsos 36% approval; Fox News 59% disapprove. UMass Lowell: 'unmitigated public-opinion disaster.' Democrats lead generic ballot 47.5% to 41.8%. 60-day War Powers deadline April 29. At least 8 WPR votes have failed (47-53 in Senate). Republican dissenters: Collins, Rand Paul, Massie. MAGA critics include Carlson, Kelly, Greene. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / AEI / Harvard / WarCosts.org / Pew / YouGov / Reuters/Ipsos / Fox News / CNN.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CEASEFIRE ON IRAN FRONT HOLDS ~144 HRS — 35,500+ cumulative — Trump: 'their military is destroyed, their whole navy is under water' — Hormuz blockade effective 14:00 GMT today — IDF 100+ Hezbollah killed at Bint Jbeil
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM Adm. Brad Cooper: 13,000+ strikes destroyed 'the military Iran built for over 40 years' — 155+ warships/submarines (largest navy elimination since WWII), 80% air defenses, 450 ballistic missile storage, 800 drone storage, 90% weapons factories, every submarine, 97% naval mines. No confirmed US/Israeli strikes on Iranian territory since 8 PM ET Apr 7 (~144 hours, ceasefire technically holds). Trump: ceasefire 'holding well,' 'their military is destroyed, their whole navy is under water.' Apr 11 Lavan Oil Refinery strike remains most recent alleged violation (attribution unclear). WSJ reports 60%+ of IRGC's naval fleet tasked with Hormuz patrol remains intact — contradicts White House claims of 97% mine elimination. Historic Apr 11: USS Frank E. Petersen Jr. (DDG-121) and USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112) transited the Strait for mine-clearing. On Lebanon front, IDF Apr 13 reports close to capturing Bint Jbeil with 100+ Hezbollah operatives killed. Israel maintains Lebanon never part of ceasefire (VP Vance: 'we never made that promise'). ~50,000+ US service members participated. Source: CENTCOM / IDF / Pentagon / USNI / WSJ.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 13 hostile KIA + 1 non-combat death — 381 WIA (CENTCOM) — Intercept disputes: 15+ KIA, 520+ injuries — no new casualties since Apr 7 ceasefire — blockade effective today raises risk
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. CENTCOM official: 13 killed in hostile action plus 1 non-combat death, 381 wounded (unchanged since Apr 8). The Intercept disputes: at least 15 KIA and 520+ total injuries including USS Ford fire casualties excluded from official counts; DCAS internal data reportedly exceeds CENTCOM's public figures by 23%. Breakdown: 7 Army hostile deaths (6 in Kuwait Port Shuaiba drone strike Mar 1, 1 from Prince Sultan AB wounds), 6 Air Force non-hostile deaths (KC-135 crash in Iraq, Mar 12). Most injuries are TBI from blast overpressure. Named KIA: Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, SFC Noah Tietjens, Capt. Cody Khork. F-15E crew (494th Fighter Squadron, callsign 'Dude 44B') fully rescued Apr 4-5 from Zagros Mountains after 36-hour evasion. No new US casualties since ceasefire (8 PM ET Apr 7). Trump's Hormuz blockade effective 14:00 GMT Apr 13 raises risk of IRGC engagement — IRGC declared any military vessel approaching the Strait a ceasefire violation to be met with 'harsh and decisive' force. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / Military Times / The Intercept.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: 381 WIA (CENTCOM) — Intercept disputes: 520+ total injuries including USS Ford fire sailors excluded — DCAS internal data reportedly 23% above CENTCOM public figures — most injuries TBI
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. CENTCOM official: 381 wounded (unchanged since Apr 8). The Intercept disputes: at least 520+ total injuries including 200+ USS Ford fire sailors excluded from official counts; DCAS internal data reportedly exceeds CENTCOM's public figures by 23%. Most injuries are traumatic brain injuries from blast overpressure. Ceasefire in effect from 8 PM ET Apr 7 (~144 hours). No confirmed new WIA since ceasefire. Trump's Hormuz blockade took effect 14:00 GMT Apr 13 — IRGC's warning that any military vessel approaching Strait will trigger 'harsh and decisive' force raises risk of new casualties. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / Military Times / The Intercept.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (est.)
iranian_killed.note: Health Min: 2,000+ / HRANA: 3,636 (1,701 civilians, 1,221 military) / Hengaw: 7,650 (1,030 civilians, 6,620 military) / IDF: 6,000+ IRGC / Gap nearly 4x between low/high estimates — no new deaths since Apr 7 ceasefire
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — deeply contested across sources with a nearly 4x spread. Iran Health Ministry (official): ~2,000+ killed, widely seen as understated. HRANA (US-based NGO) Apr 7: 3,636 total (1,701 civilians, 1,221 military) — named verification. Hengaw (Norway-based) Apr 8: 7,650 total (1,030 civilians, 6,620 military) — field reporting, more than double HRANA driven almost entirely by military casualty estimates. IDF claims 6,000+ IRGC killed. Iran's internet blackout severely limits independent verification. HRANA acknowledges military casualties are 'significantly higher' than its confirmed count. No Iranian deaths reported since ceasefire took effect Apr 7. Combined extrapolated estimate: ~9,100+. OCHA Apr 3: 115,193 civilian units damaged, 763 schools, 334 health/emergency centers. WHO verified 23 attacks on healthcare in Iran. Pasteur Institute (Iran's premier public health facility) severely damaged. Four Tehran refineries set ablaze Mar 7. Qeshm Island desalination plant strike cut water to 30 villages. Iran executed at least 14 protesters between Mar 17 and Apr 6 including teen wrestler Saleh Mohammadi and 18-year-old Amir Hossein Hatami (Hengaw). UNHCR $80M appeal for 2.8M affected people in Iran. Source: Iran Health Ministry / HRANA / Hengaw / IDF / OCHA / WHO / UNHCR.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 2,055 killed, 6,588 wounded (Lebanon Public Health EOC via CGTN Apr 12) — 1.2M displaced — 100+ Hezbollah killed at Bint Jbeil — Red Cross paramedic killed by IDF drone Apr 13 — talks Apr 15
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli operations since March 2. Cumulative (Lebanon Public Health Emergency Operation Center via CGTN Apr 12): 2,055 killed, 6,588 wounded — up from 2,020/6,436 on Day 44. Displacement: 1.2M+ (14% of population); 200,000+ fled to Syria. Apr 12: Hezbollah launched ~20 rockets + drones at Kfar Yuval, Yir'on, Kiryat Shmona. IDF raided Bint Jbeil Governmental Hospital, killing ~20 Hezbollah operatives. Netanyahu visited southern Lebanon with Defense Minister Katz and IDF Chief Zamir, declaring 'the war continues.' Katz announced Operation 'Silver Plough' to demolish homes along the border. Israeli forces rammed UNIFIL vehicles with a Merkava tank on two occasions. Apr 13: Sirens sounded across Galilee for drone attacks including near Acre. IDF reports close to capturing Bint Jbeil (Hezbollah's historic stronghold) with 100+ Hezbollah operatives killed. A Lebanese Red Cross paramedic killed by Israeli drone that 'directly targeted' the team (NBC News). Apr 8 'Operation Eternal Darkness' remains deadliest day. Lebanon-Israel talks confirmed Tuesday Apr 15 at US State Department (first Leiter-Moawad phone call Apr 10-11). Israel insists talks will NOT include ceasefire; Hezbollah's parliamentary bloc rejected direct talks. PM Salam demanded ceasefire as precondition and postponed Washington trip. Nine Litani River bridges destroyed; sole lifeline Qasmieh Bridge serves 71,000+. WHO: 77 attacks on healthcare, 53 health worker deaths. Food prices spiking: vegetables +20%, bread +17% MoM. Source: Lebanon Public Health EOC / CGTN / IDF / Netanyahu / NBC News / WHO / UNHCR.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: 7.8M+ — Iran 3.2M+ IDPs (compounded by flooding in 24 provinces) — Lebanon 1.2M+ (200K+ in Syria) — 55,500 Afghan refugees returned — WHO Dubai hub ON HOLD — UNHCR $80M appeal for Iran
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. Iran: 3.2 million+ internally displaced (compounded by severe flooding in 24 provinces). 55,500 Afghan refugees returned to Afghanistan from Iran. 4.4M Afghans in Iran particularly vulnerable. Lebanon: 1.2 million+ displaced (14% of population); 200,000+ fled to Syria. Humanitarian access is collapsing. WHO's Dubai logistics hub — the global health emergency hub — is ON HOLD due to regional insecurity, delaying 50+ emergency supply requests from 25 countries. Strait of Hormuz closure disrupted 80%+ of GCC food imports. UNHCR has issued an $80 million appeal for 2.8 million affected people in Iran. Iran continued executing protesters during the war — at least 14 confirmed secret executions Mar 17-Apr 6 including teen wrestler Saleh Mohammadi and 18-year-old Amir Hossein Hatami (Hengaw). Ceasefire on Iran front holds ~144 hours but Lebanon excluded — continued Israeli operations. Source: UNHCR / WHO / OCHA / UNICEF / IFRC / Hengaw.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — HORMUZ BLOCKADE EFFECTIVE 14:00 GMT TODAY — ~3,200 vessels stranded (800 tankers, 20,000 mariners) — IRGC warns military vessels approaching trigger 'harsh' force — UK declines to join
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28. Hormuz functionally closed. Normal traffic averages 138 transits/day; current volume fewer than 10. Approximately 3,200 vessels stranded in/near the Gulf, including 800 tankers and 20,000 mariners. War risk insurance jumped from 0.125% to ~1% of ship value. All major shipping lines (Maersk, CMA CGM, Hapag-Lloyd) suspended transits. Iran publicly acknowledged mines in the Strait — unprecedented — and claims to have 'lost track' of some. Trump's US naval blockade took effect 14:00 GMT Apr 13. CENTCOM narrowed scope to Iranian ports only (not all Hormuz traffic) — a significant divergence from Trump's Truth Social announcement. IRGC declared any military vessel approaching the Strait a ceasefire violation to be met with 'harsh and decisive' force. IRGC: 'Security in the Persian Gulf and the Sea of Oman is either for everyone or for NO ONE' / 'NO PORT in the region will be safe.' Coalition support thin — UK explicitly declined (Starmer: 'not getting dragged into the war'); Australia not asked. Apr 11 historic transit: USS Frank E. Petersen Jr. (DDG-121) and USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112) initiated mine-clearing. CENTCOM: 'additional U.S. forces, including underwater drones, will join the clearance effort.' Source: ADNOC / Lloyd's List / Bloomberg / IMO / IATA / CENTCOM / Trump Truth Social.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: HRANA Iran: 244+ — UNICEF Lebanon: 600 killed or injured — Pope Leo XIV departs Rome Apr 13 for Africa — responds to Trump attacks: 'Blessed are the peacemakers'
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. HRANA: 244+ children killed in Iran (15% of casualties under 18). UNICEF reports 600 children killed or injured in Lebanon. April 8 'Operation Eternal Darkness': 254-357 killed including 33 children. Key atrocities: Minab school attack (Feb 28, ~175 killed including schoolchildren) — HRW called it a war crime; Lamerd sports hall (4 children); Karaj bridge (8 civilians). 763 schools damaged, 65+ destroyed. 30+ universities struck. Pope Leo XIV led a 10,000-person Prayer Vigil for Peace at St. Peter's Basilica Apr 11, warning of the 'delusion of omnipotence' fueling war. Apr 13: Pope departed Rome for 11-day Africa trip, pushed back on Trump attacks from papal plane: 'I will not enter into debate... I have no fear of neither the Trump administration nor of speaking out loudly about the message of the Gospel. Blessed are the peacemakers.' Trump had attacked him Apr 12 on Truth Social: 'Pope Leo is WEAK on Crime, and terrible for Foreign Policy... If I wasn't in the White House, Leo wouldn't be in the Vatican.' Iran executions during war include teen wrestler Saleh Mohammadi. Source: UNICEF / HRANA / Lebanese MoH / WHO / HRW / Amnesty International / Vatican / Hengaw.
